mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 1.5 million signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in deteriorating labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that reduces corporate profitability and increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals an improving consumer environment that increases demand for equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in improving consumer environment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an improving housing market that increases demand for equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in improving housing market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that increases corporate profitability and reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals an inflationary environment that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in inflationary environment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a supply-side shock that reduces corporate profitability and increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
